ownerPhrase: My dog is scooting on carpet after going outside.
notes: Missing family baseline only; does not claim current runtime support.

ownerPhrase: She keeps butt licking and seems bothered back there.
notes: Missing family baseline only; does not claim current runtime support.

ownerPhrase: There is a foul smell near rear end and he keeps turning around.
notes: Weak owner-language concern should be retained as an unknown concern instead of being dropped.
noOpUnsafeBecause: A no-op would lose a localized discomfort or discharge clue before intake can ask a broad follow-up.

ownerPhrase: My dog is drooling a lot and won't settle.
notes: Weakly mapped owner phrase should fall back to an unknown concern with safety screening.
noOpUnsafeBecause: A no-op would drop an ingestion, mouth, or breathing-adjacent clue that may need fast screening.
safetyReason: Heavy drooling with restlessness needs an emergency screen before intake handles it as low signal.

ownerPhrase: He keeps eating rocks in the yard.
notes: Missing family baseline only; does not claim current runtime support.

ownerPhrase: My dog ate a sock this morning.
notes: Missing family baseline only; does not claim current runtime support.
safetyReason: Swallowed-object language needs an emergency screen instead of disappearing from intake.

ownerPhrase: She has started eating dirt from the planter.
notes: Missing family baseline only; does not claim current runtime support.

ownerPhrase: My dog is head pressing against the wall.
notes: Missing family baseline only; does not claim current runtime support.
safetyReason: Head-pressing language should trigger emergency screening before intake continues.

ownerPhrase: He keeps circling the room and seems confused.
notes: Missing family baseline only; does not claim current runtime support.
safetyReason: Circling with confusion needs emergency screening rather than silent fallback.

ownerPhrase: She is staring at wall for long stretches.
notes: Ambiguous behavior phrase needs intake clarification before any family claim.
safetyReason: Clarification is needed to separate normal fixation from a concerning behavior change.

ownerPhrase: My dog has an arched back and walks stiffly.
notes: Posture language is weakly mapped and should be retained for follow-up.
noOpUnsafeBecause: A no-op would drop a pain-adjacent posture clue before intake asks where and when it started.
safetyReason: Clarification is needed because posture plus stiffness may reflect a time-sensitive pain pattern.

ownerPhrase: He is reluctant to move and just stands in one place.
notes: General movement reluctance should not be dropped when it fails direct mapping.
noOpUnsafeBecause: A no-op would lose a mobility or weakness clue before the owner can clarify severity.
safetyReason: Clarification is needed to distinguish mild hesitation from severe mobility change.

ownerPhrase: His appetite increased a lot this week.
notes: Missing family baseline only; does not claim current runtime support.

ownerPhrase: My dog is always hungry even after meals.
notes: Missing family baseline only; does not claim current runtime support.

ownerPhrase: She has sudden weight gain and seems heavier around the middle.
notes: Missing family baseline only; does not claim current runtime support.

ownerPhrase: My dog is having trouble swallowing his food.
notes: Swallowing language needs at least unknown-concern capture when direct mapping is absent.
noOpUnsafeBecause: A no-op would lose an airway or mouth-adjacent clue before screening can happen.
safetyReason: Trouble swallowing should be screened for urgent breathing, choking, or distress signals.

ownerPhrase: He is gagging when swallowing water.
notes: Gagging tied to swallowing should not be dropped by symptom intake.
noOpUnsafeBecause: A no-op would miss a swallow-linked distress clue before intake can screen severity.
safetyReason: Gagging while swallowing needs emergency screening for breathing or choking-adjacent signs.

ownerPhrase: There is a paw pad cut after our walk.
notes: Missing family baseline only; does not claim current runtime support.

ownerPhrase: My dog is limping after stepping on something sharp.
notes: Already maps to the broad limping symptom, but the object-step detail is worth preserving for future coverage.

ownerPhrase: She has a broken nail and is holding up the paw.
notes: Missing family baseline only; does not claim current runtime support.

ownerPhrase: His tail limp and hanging lower than usual.
notes: Missing family baseline only; does not claim current runtime support.

ownerPhrase: He yelps when I touch the tail base pain area.
notes: Missing family baseline only; does not claim current runtime support.

ownerPhrase: My dog's voice changed and his bark sounds different.
notes: Voice change is ambiguous without breathing, swallow, or cough detail.
safetyReason: Clarification is needed to decide whether airway-adjacent screening should happen.

ownerPhrase: My dog has noisy breathing / stridor when resting.
notes: Already maps to the broad breathing symptom; this phrase should remain an emergency watch case.
safetyReason: Noisy breathing at rest needs emergency screening before normal intake flow.

ownerPhrase: My dog has acute deafness and suddenly is not reacting to sounds.
notes: Missing family baseline only; does not claim current runtime support.
safetyReason: Sudden hearing change needs clarification and associated-sign screening.
